severity_policy.blocking: The PR should not merge because changed behavior is broken, unsafe, or violates a hard repository boundary.
severity_policy.high: A likely production or data-loss regression, security/privacy issue, or serious runtime/storage contract risk.
severity_policy.medium: A concrete bug, edge case, maintainability trap, or missing proof tied directly to changed behavior.
severity_policy.low: A small but actionable correctness, proof, or maintainability risk tied to changed behavior.
severity_policy.nitpick: Optional changed-line polish with no behavior risk, such as readability, naming, tiny duplication, stale comments, dead code, type clarity, or local consistency.

nitpick_policy.risk: No behavior-risk requirement; use for optional polish only.

control_warn_types.Proof Gap: Important changed behavior lacks focused proof.
control_warn_types.Review Limitation: Bunny lacked full packet/context to prove a suspected issue.
control_warn_types.CI Timing: Expected checks were missing, pending, or not yet observable when posted.
control_warn_types.Non-blocking Coverage: Useful coverage or context note that should not block merge by itself.

path_instructions.name: Engine and source lanes
checks: Engine code stays React-free and does not import feature internals, runtime adapters, Tauri APIs, or browser APIs.
Feature code renders UI and calls engine helpers plus runtime/shared API wrappers rather than raw host or remote transports.
Storage, generation, secrets, files, provider transport, and import/export stay separated by their documented owners.

path_instructions.name: Storage, import, and runtime contracts
checks: TypeScript command/entity declarations stay aligned with Rust desktop and runtime command registrations.
Collection names and native DeKoi record shapes remain the contract.
Legacy compatibility stays one-way import behavior into native DeKoi records.

path_instructions.name: Clean-room and docs
checks: Docs, prompts, config, and templates use DeKoi-owned terms unless an authored workflow document is intentionally being reused.
No old source code, assets, generated bindings, UI text, schemas, or component layouts are copied into product code.
Workflow changes remain concrete, testable, and narrow.
